content:
Hi everyone,

I'm looking to write Rust kernel modules for a hardware and I'm interested in knowing how to write character and platform device drivers.

I noticed that the outdated documentation from [Rust-for-Linux] (https://rust-for-linux.github.io/docs/kernel/index.html#modules) provides more modules than the actual documentation in the kernel.

So my questions are

  • How can I implement the common device driver types in Rust using the current documentation?
  • If there are no abstractions available anywhere, where can I learn how to write abstractions and bindings?

Best Regards,
Franc

Abstractions for miscdevices are landing in v6.13 through the char-misc-next tree. You can find docs for them under the docs for linux-next: https://rust.docs.kernel.org/next/kernel/miscdevice/index.html
Please also have a look to Danilo's Device / Driver PCI / Platform Rust abstractions. Esp. to the samples/rust/rust_driver_platform.rs in patch 16/16.

Alice Ryhl said:

Abstractions for miscdevices are landing in v6.13 through the char-misc-next tree. You can find docs for them under the docs for linux-next: https://rust.docs.kernel.org/next/kernel/miscdevice/index.html

Alice, do you have any samples for the miscdevice? Trying to figure out how to use the abstraction correctly.

Charalampos Mitrodimas said:

any samples for the miscdevice?

Does samples: rust: Provide example using the new Rust MiscDevice abstraction from the mailing list help?
Thank you both @Alice Ryhl @Dirk Behme . Both are excellent.
